hero.subline: A rare opportunity to acquire land in an exclusive religious moshav in the heart of the Martyrs' Forest. For families seeking a home — not a property.

promise.line1: Some places are sold through brochures.
promise.line2: Beit Meir is not one of them.
promise.body: One of the oldest and most respected religious moshavim in the Jerusalem corridor — a community of around 200 families, a 200-year-old forest just beyond the window, and twenty minutes from central Jerusalem. Opportunities to acquire land here are rare, and most transactions happen quietly.

gallery.heading: In the Heart of the Martyrs' Forest
gallery.para1: In the heart of the Martyrs' Forest, on the historic road from Tel Aviv to Jerusalem, lies Moshav Beit Meir.
gallery.para2: Founded in 1950 by religious immigrants from Hungary and Romania as part of the Mizrachi movement, the moshav was named after Rabbi Meir Bar-Ilan. The historic Burma Road — the road that saved Jerusalem during the War of Independence — passes alongside it.
gallery.para3: Today, Beit Meir is a tight-knit religious community of about 200 families. Its setting — the HaMasrek Reserve with its 170-year-old Jerusalem pines, and dozens of hiking trails winding through the Judean Hills — turns every morning here into something else entirely. The vineyards of a local winery climb the hillsides around the moshav, a quiet testament to the soil, the mountain air, and the climate that set this region apart.

community.quote: We weren't looking for a house. We were looking for a place. Beit Meir is that place.

pillars.title: Established Religious Community
pillars.body: Founded by Mizrachi pioneers, continuous religious life since 1950, Bnei Akiva, multiple shuls and yeshivot nearby — Yeshivat Harei Yehuda, Ohr Yerushalayim, Ramot Shapira Educational Center.

pillars.title: Education at the Highest Level
pillars.body: Walking distance to yeshiva, short drive to top religious schools in Jerusalem and Beit Shemesh. The Ramot Shapira Educational Center is located on the moshav itself.

pillars.title: Land, Forest, and Vine
pillars.body: At ~650m elevation in the Jerusalem Hills, surrounded by the protected Martyrs' Forest, dozens of hiking trails out the back gate, and the vineyards of a local winery climbing the hillsides. The same soil and air that produce wine of this quality have raised generations of families here.

opportunity.heading: Land in a moshav like this does not sit on the open market.
opportunity.body: Conversations happen quietly, between people who know people. If you are reading these lines, it is likely we have information that will interest you. Completing the form below is the first step. A member of our team will reach out to you personally within 24 hours.

about.founding_body: In 1950, religious immigrants from Hungary and Romania — pioneers of the Mizrachi movement — laid the foundations of Moshav Beit Meir. The moshav was named in honor of Rabbi Meir Bar-Ilan, one of the great leaders of religious Zionism and founder of the Mizrachi movement. From its earliest days, Beit Meir maintained its tight-knit religious character — a community built on the values of Torah, labor, and love of the Land.
about.burma_body: Adjacent to the moshav runs the Burma Road — one of the great stories of the War of Independence. In 1948, when Jerusalem was under siege, an alternative route was carved through the mountains that allowed the siege to be broken and supplies to reach the city. Beit Meir stood at the heart of that story. The historic road now runs as a marked trail alongside the moshav — and those who walk it feel the weight of history with every step.
about.land_body: The moshav sits in the heart of the Martyrs' Forest — a forest planted by the Jewish National Fund in memory of the victims of the Holocaust. Jerusalem pines 170 years old surround the moshav on all sides. The adjacent HaMasrek Reserve contains some of the oldest groves in the country. This is not merely beautiful surroundings — it is land that carries meaning.
about.nature_body: The moshav sits in the heart of one of Israel's most distinguished natural environments. The adjacent HaMasrek Reserve contains Jerusalem pines 170 years old — one of the oldest pine groves in the country. Dozens of marked hiking trails begin literally from the back gate of the moshav and wind through the Judean Hills. The vineyards of a local winery climb the hillsides around the moshav — a quiet testament to the quality of the soil, the mountain air, and the climate that set this region apart.
about.today_body: Beit Meir today is a tight-knit religious community of about 200 families. Its residents — professionals, doctors, lawyers, business owners — commute to Jerusalem and beyond for work, and return in the evening to the quiet of the mountains. Turnover is low. People do not leave Beit Meir.

location_page.hero_heading: At the Heart of the Land
location_page.hero_subheading: Close to everything that matters. Far from everything that doesn't.
location_page.how_body: Exit Highway 1 at the Shoresh interchange, then take road 3955 toward Beit Meir. The drive from Jerusalem takes approximately 20 minutes. Bus lines 186, 615, and 617 connect the moshav to Jerusalem and Beit Shemesh.

lifestyle_page.climate_body: Beit Meir sits at approximately 650 meters elevation in the Judean Hills. Mediterranean mountain climate — cool nights even in summer (when Tel Aviv is a humid 28°C, Beit Meir is a dry 22°C). Mild winters — average January 9–12°C — with occasional snowfall. Around 600mm of rain per year, concentrated November–March. Approximately 3,400 hours of sunshine annually.
lifestyle_page.nature_heading: Step Out the Door — Into a Forest
lifestyle_page.nature_body: The moshav sits in the heart of the Martyrs' Forest, surrounded by some of the most beautiful protected nature in Israel. The adjacent HaMasrek Reserve contains Jerusalem pines 170 years old — one of the oldest pine groves in the country. Dozens of marked hiking trails begin literally from the back gate of the moshav: the historic Burma Road trail, Sataf trails with ancient agricultural terraces and springs, Ein Kesalon spring, Nahal Kesalon, the Israel National Trail passes nearby, and routes connecting to Ein Hemed and Sho'eva. For residents, 'going for a walk' means stepping out the door into a forest.
lifestyle_page.wine_body: The hillsides surrounding Beit Meir host the vineyards of a local winery — a quiet but meaningful detail. Wineries do not plant in just any soil. The combination of altitude (650m), cool nights, mountain air, well-drained limestone-rich earth, and the particular Mediterranean-mountain microclimate makes this region one of Israel's distinguished wine-growing zones. The Judean Hills wine appellation has earned international recognition over the past two decades. The same earth and the same air that grow grapes worth bottling have raised the people who live here.
lifestyle_page.day_body: Shacharit at the local shul with neighbors who have become friends. A drive to a meeting in Jerusalem before the traffic builds. Back for lunch. An afternoon walk through the pines with the dog, children running between the trees. Friday evening — a Shabbat table with neighbors, windows open, the scent of forest. This is not a metaphor. This is what happens here every week.
lifestyle_page.air_body: At this elevation, this far from Tel Aviv's light pollution, the night sky is genuinely visible. In summer, sleeping with the windows open is not a metaphor — it is what people do here. Noise levels, air quality, and quiet are real luxury indicators in the modern Israeli context.

community_page.hero_subheading: Families from around the world who chose to build their lives in the Land of Israel — together.
community_page.intro: Beit Meir is a unique religious-Zionist community. Here you will find families from the US, UK, Canada, France, and across the world, all sharing one dream: to live a full Jewish life in the Land of Israel, in a natural setting, within a warm community.

values.title: Judaism
values.body: A full Jewish life — Shabbat, holidays, prayer, and Torah study.

values.title: Zionism
values.body: Love of the Land and its building — not merely as an ideal, but as daily reality.

values.title: Family
values.body: A community that honors and strengthens the family.

values.title: Education
values.body: Children grow up with values, excellent schooling, and deep roots.

values.title: Nature
values.body: Living in nature, not near it. Forests, trails, and mountain air.

values.title: Quiet
values.body: Far from the noise, close to everything that matters.

testimonials.quote: We weren't looking for a house. We were looking for a place. Beit Meir is that place.

testimonials.name: The Levy Family

testimonials.name: The Shapiro Family

community_page.cta_body: Leave your details and we will be happy to tell you more.
community_page.religious_body: Beit Meir has maintained its unique religious character since its founding in 1950. A central synagogue with regular minyanim, eruv, kosher infrastructure, active Bnei Akiva, and the atmosphere of a community that built itself on the values of Torah and labor. This is not a 'religious neighborhood' — it is a moshav born of faith and that has preserved it.
community_page.education_body: The Ramot Shapira Educational Center is located on the moshav itself. Yeshivat Harei Yehuda and Ohr Yerushalayim are nearby. Leading religious schools in Jerusalem and Beit Shemesh are a 20-minute drive. For families for whom education is the first priority, Beit Meir offers an infrastructure that is difficult to find elsewhere.
community_page.demographics_body: About 200 established families — doctors, lawyers, business owners, academics. People who chose to live here deliberately. Turnover is very low — people do not leave Beit Meir. This is one of the best indicators of community quality.

testimonials.quote: We weren't looking for a house. We were looking for a place. Beit Meir is that place.
testimonials.author: Resident, 14 years in the moshav

testimonials.quote: We made aliyah and wanted to build a real home — not an apartment. When we saw Beit Meir, we understood this was what we had been looking for. The community, the nature, the proximity to Jerusalem — everything.
testimonials.author: Oleh family, 8 years in the moshav

testimonials.quote: My children grew up here with friends for life. This is not a property — it is a legacy.
testimonials.author: Long-time resident, 22 years in the moshav

privacy_page.heading: Privacy Policy

sections.heading: 1. Introduction
sections.body: Moshav Beit Meir ('we', 'our') respects your privacy. This policy explains how we collect, use, and protect the information you provide to us.

sections.heading: 2. Information We Collect
sections.body: We collect information you provide directly through the contact form: full name, phone number, email address, and preferred contact method. We also collect technical information such as IP address, browser type, and usage data.

sections.heading: 3. Use of Information
sections.body:
The information collected will be used to:
• Contact you regarding your inquiry
• Improve our service
• Send marketing updates (with your consent only)

sections.heading: 4. Sharing of Information
sections.body: We do not sell, rent, or share your personal information with third parties, except as necessary to provide the service (such as Airtable for lead management) and as required by law.

sections.heading: 5. Cookies
sections.body: The site uses cookies to improve your browsing experience. You can manage cookie settings in your browser.

sections.heading: 6. Data Security
sections.body: We take reasonable security measures to protect your information, including SSL encryption.

sections.heading: 7. Your Rights
sections.body: You have the right to view, correct, and delete the information we hold about you. To contact us: info@beit-meir.co.il

sections.heading: 8. Contact
sections.body: For any questions regarding this privacy policy, please contact us at: info@beit-meir.co.il

privacy_page.intro: Moshav Beit Meir ('we', 'our') respects your privacy. This policy explains how we collect, use, and protect the information you provide to us.
privacy_page.collection_body: We collect information you provide directly through the contact form: full name, phone number, email address, and preferred contact method. This information is used solely to contact you regarding land acquisition opportunities in Moshav Beit Meir.
privacy_page.use_body: Your information will not be sold, transferred, or shared with third parties for marketing purposes. It will be used solely for personal contact regarding your inquiry.
privacy_page.cookies_body: We use Plausible Analytics — a privacy-friendly analytics system that does not collect personal information and does not require GDPR consent. We may use Meta Pixel for targeted advertising — if so, a notice will be displayed at the bottom of the site.
privacy_page.rights_body: You may at any time request to view, correct, or delete the information you have provided. For inquiries: contact us through the site form.
privacy_page.contact_body: For questions regarding the privacy policy, contact us through the contact form.
